LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS UNUM GROUP, UNUMPROVIDENT CORPORATION, UNUM CORPORATION, NATIONAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, PROVIDENT LIFE AND ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY AND UNUM LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED; PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 3 AS TO THE ADEQUACY OF DEFENDANT'S INVESTIGATION GIVEN EVIDENCE FROM WHICH A TRIER OF FACT COULD REASONABLY CONCLUDE THAT DEFENDANT HAD REASON TO KNOW OR AT LEAST TO FURTHER INVESTIGATE WHETHER PLAINTIFF SUFFERED LIMITATIONS IN THE OPINION OF HIS TREATING PHYSICIAN. DEFENDANT'S HAVE NOT CARRIED THEIR BURDEN WITH RESPECT TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT DENNIS JAFFE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED, IF MOVING PARTY PROVIDES A PROOF OF SERVICE EVIDENCING PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE OF SERVICE BY NOON OF MAY-28-2010 OTHERWISE IT WILL BE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189 * * * PROOF OF SERVICE WAS FILED ON MAY-28-2010 BUT COURTESY COPY WAS LODGED ON JUN-1-2010. DEFENDANT DENIS JAFFE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. A VALID PROOF OF SERVICE WAS FILED. MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. * * *

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS UNUM GROUP, UNUMPROVIDENT CORPORATION, UNUM CORPORATION, NATIONAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, PROVIDENT LIFE AND ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY AND UNUM LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED; PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 3 AS TO THE ADEQUACY OF DEFENDANT'S INVESTIGATION GIVEN EVIDENCE FROM WHICH A TRIER OF FACT COULD REASONABLY CONCLUDE THAT DEFENDANT HAD REASON TO KNOW OR AT LEAST TO FURTHER INVESTIGATE WHETHER PLAINTIFF SUFFERED LIMITATIONS IN THE OPINION OF HIS TREATING PHYSICIAN. DEFENDANT'S HAVE NOT CARRIED THEIR BURDEN WITH RESPECT TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD.
